constraints: npm permissions, rate limits, package availability, and service policies still apply
Published tarball inspection is bounded to 50 MB compressed and 20,000 entries
human_boundaries: Account creation, login, passkeys, recovery, and two-factor challenges require the user's credentials and human authorization.
Adding or removing distribution tags changes what users install and requires package ownership.
Changing package owners grants or revokes publication authority and requires privileged account access.
Deprecating or undeprecating releases changes public install warnings and requires package ownership.
Organization members, teams, roles, and billing require organization-owner access unavailable to the public matrix.
Package visibility and team access changes require authenticated package or organization administration.
Pro and Teams purchases, invoices, and payment changes are financial human-only boundaries.
Profile and email changes require an authenticated npm account and may require password or two-factor confirmation.
Publishing changes the public software supply chain and requires package contents, ownership, and narrowly scoped human confirmation.
Staged publication requires an authenticated publisher account and a controlled disposable test package.
Starring and unstarring packages changes personalized account state and requires a dedicated signed-in mapping account.
Submitting support or malware reports sends user-provided information and requires case-specific human approval.
Token creation and revocation expose sensitive credentials and require authenticated human authorization.
Unpublishing is destructive, time-limited, and requires package ownership plus explicit human confirmation.
coverage.website_parity: All anonymous public npm package, registry, downloads, package-content, dependents, documentation, policy, and status surfaces in the declared en-US matrix have reviewed browserless operations. Account and publisher roles remain visible outside that matrix.

description: Complete browserless interface for npm's anonymous public registry and website surfaces: discovery, metadata, releases, dependencies, dependents, downloads, provenance, security, package contents, documentation, and service health.

get_provenance.summary: Return bounded npm publish and SLSA provenance attestations with transparency-log identifiers.

assertions.name: provenance lookup returns an attestation collection for the resolved release

get_provenance.when_not_to_use: Do not interpret provenance as proof that package code is safe; it only links publication to recorded build identity.
get_provenance.when_to_use: Use this to establish whether a release carries npm provenance and where its attestations were logged.

assertions.name: README lookup reports bounded content and truncation

get_readme.when_not_to_use: Do not use this for npm's own documentation; read_site_resource handles official npm pages.
get_readme.when_to_use: Use this for package usage, examples, migration notes, and author-provided documentation.

get_registry_status.summary: Return npm's current overall status, component states, and active public incidents.

assertions.name: registry health returns npm's current status indicator

get_registry_status.when_not_to_use: Do not use this as proof that a specific package exists or that an account has permission to access it.
get_registry_status.when_to_use: Use this when package, downloads, authentication, or website operations fail unexpectedly.

get_version.when_to_use: Use this when an agent needs the install-time facts for one package release or tag.

assertions.name: dependency lookup returns a paginated normalized collection

list_dependencies.when_to_use: Use this to inspect dependency requirements without downloading or installing the package.

list_dependents.when_not_to_use: Do not treat the order or total as a permanent registry invariant because npm continuously refreshes this index.
list_dependents.when_to_use: Use this to estimate downstream ecosystem impact or discover projects built on a package.

list_maintainer_packages.summary: List public packages associated with an npm maintainer account using the registry's maintained search index.

assertions.name: maintainer lookup returns packages for the requested account

list_maintainer_packages.when_to_use: Use this to inspect a maintainer's public package portfolio without opening the npm profile page.

list_package_files.summary: Inspect a published npm tarball and list its files, sizes, modes, and entry types without extracting it.

assertions.name: tarball inspection returns bounded safe paths

list_package_files.when_not_to_use: Do not use this for packages over the documented inspection limits; download_tarball returns a local file receipt instead.
list_package_files.when_to_use: Use this to reproduce npm's Code tab or locate a file before calling read_package_file.

list_versions.when_not_to_use: Do not request this when only latest is needed; get_package is smaller and faster.
list_versions.when_to_use: Use this to inspect release history, find an older version, or compare stable and prerelease publication activity.

content.description: Complete bounded file content in the declared encoding.

read_package_file.summary: Read one bounded regular file directly from a public npm tarball as UTF-8 text or base64.

assertions.name: package file read returns content with a cryptographic digest

read_package_file.when_not_to_use: Do not request directories, links, unsafe paths, or files larger than the selected byte limit.
read_package_file.when_to_use: Use this after list_package_files to inspect package manifests, source, licenses, or bundled documentation.

read_site_resource.summary: Read a bounded public npmjs.com policy, product, help, or docs.npmjs.com page without opening a browser.

assertions.name: site resource read returns bounded visible text

read_site_resource.when_not_to_use: Do not use it for arbitrary internet URLs or package READMEs; only official npm pages are accepted.
read_site_resource.when_to_use: Use this for npm documentation and policy URLs that are not package records.

search_packages.name: search_packages

total.description: Total matching packages reported by npm.

pagination.behavior: Pass the numeric next_cursor as offset on the next call.

result.freshness: Live public npm data with bounded local caching appropriate to the resource.
result.truncation: Collections, page text, README content, tarballs, and package files report explicit bounds or pagination.

search_packages.summary: Search the public npm registry with npm's text, keyword, maintainer, and scope query syntax.

assertions.name: search returns a bounded package collection and total

search_packages.when_not_to_use: Do not use it when the exact package name is already known; get_package is faster and more complete.
search_packages.when_to_use: Use this to discover packages, compare likely candidates, or enumerate a maintainer, scope, or keyword.
